name: Axe Mastery
desc: You can use the Push and Sweep mastery properties with axes.

name: Sword Mastery
desc: You can use the Push, Sweep, and Topple mastery properties with swords.

name: Polearm Mastery
desc: You can use the Reach and Sweep mastery properties with polearms.

name: Bow Mastery
desc: You can make ranged attacks with bows without penalty for being within 5 feet of a hostile creature.
benefits: No penalty for close ranged attacks with bows

name: Unarmed Mastery
desc: Your unarmed strikes deal 1d6 bludgeoning damage and can be used with the Trip and Push mastery properties.

name: Two-Weapon Fighting
desc: When you make an attack with a light weapon, you can use a bonus action to make one attack with a different light weapon you are holding in your other hand.
benefits: Bonus action attack with second light weapon

name: Archery Fighting Style
desc: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons.
benefits: +2 to attack rolls with ranged weapons

name: Defense Fighting Style
desc: While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

name: Dueling Fighting Style
desc: When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.
benefits: +2 to damage with one-handed melee weapons

name: Great Weapon Fighting Style
desc: When you roll a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack you make with a two-handed or versatile weapon, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.
benefits: Reroll 1s and 2s on damage dice for two-handed weapons
